Package nonprofitbookkeeping.ui.actions.scaledger

Class SCALedgerDataLoader

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.ui.actions.scaledger.SCALedgerDataLoader
public class SCALedgerDataLoader extends Object
Utility class for loading data from SCA formatted Excel spreadsheets into Java objects using JXLS Reader. It uses an XML mapping file to define how data from the Excel sheet corresponds to Java bean properties.

    loadData(File mappingFile, File currentInputFile)
    Loads data from a specified Excel file (.xlsx or .xlsm) into a map of Java beans using a JXLS XML mapping file.

  • Constructor Details

    • SCALedgerDataLoader

      public SCALedgerDataLoader()

  • Method Details

    • loadData

      public static Map<String,Object> loadData(File mappingFile, File currentInputFile) throws Exception
      Loads data from a specified Excel file (.xlsx or .xlsm) into a map of Java beans using a JXLS XML mapping file.

      This method initializes LedgerMetadata, Summary, and LedgerContainer (with its quarterly ledger lists) and puts them into a "beans" map. The JXLS XLSReader then populates these beans based on the rules defined in the mappingFile and data from the currentInputFile. Error skipping is enabled in the JXLS reader configuration.

      Parameters:
      mappingFile - The JXLS XML mapping File that defines how to read the Excel data into the Java beans.
      currentInputFile - The Excel File (.xlsx or .xlsm) containing the SCA ledger data.
      Returns:
      A Map where keys are bean names (e.g., "metadata", "summary", "ledgerQ1") and values are the populated Java bean objects.
      Throws:
      SAXException - if there is an error parsing the JXLS mapping XML file.
      FileNotFoundException - if either mappingFile or currentInputFile is not found.
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs during reading of the files or JXLS processing.
      IllegalStateException - if JXLS fails to read the Excel file successfully (XLSReadStatus.isStatusOK() is false).
      Exception - for other potential errors during JXLS processing.
